adversarial_verdict: This plan will execute to completion in a clean dev environment. Applied fixes address the most severe prior issues. Remaining vulnerabilities: Pydantic model allows angle_target_degrees=0 while backend rejects it; create_session accepts invalid process_type without validation; prototype scripts (prototype_arc_scoring.py, prototype_aggregate_perf.py) are not updated and will score TIG sessions with MIG constants; migration is never run in CI (tests use SQLite + create_all); and flicker verification is subjective.

sabotage_vectors.attack: WeldTypeThresholds has angle_target_degrees: ge=0 (allows 0). Backend PUT rejects 0 in Step 1.5. A test or script instantiates WeldTypeThresholds(angle_target_degrees=0, ...) and passes it to extract_features/score_session. Every angle deviation from 0 triggers warning; scoring silently wrong.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Verification only tests 'Field validation rejects negative values'. Zero is not tested. Instantiation with 0 passes.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: extract_features with angle_target_deg=0 produces wrong angle_max_deviation; all angle-based scoring is wrong

sabotage_vectors.attack: Pre-flight runs psql $DATABASE_URL for manual UPDATE when sessions>1000. If DATABASE_URL points to a read replica, UPDATE fails with permission error. Operator assumes migration blocked for other reason.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: No step verifies database is writable. Pre-flight checklist has no read-only check.

sabotage_vectors.attack: Migration 004 runs only on real PostgreSQL. Tests use in-memory SQLite with Base.metadata.create_all. Alembic upgrade is never executed in CI. PostgreSQL-specific migration failure (e.g. op.execute syntax, lock timeout) goes undetected until production deploy.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: No CI step runs alembic upgrade head against Postgres or even SQLite. test_thresholds_api creates tables from models, not migrations.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: First production migration run may fail; schema drift if partial

sabotage_vectors.attack: create_session accepts process_type in CreateSessionRequest with no validation. Client POSTs process_type='submerged_arc' or 'typo_tig'. Session stored; get_thresholds falls back to mig; session scored with MIG. Wrong process type persists in DB; no audit trail.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Verification checks POST accepts process_type and GET returns it. Invalid values pass both. No validation that process_type in (mig,tig,stick,flux_core).
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: All scoring for incorrectly-typed sessions uses MIG thresholds; WelderReport callout shows wrong spec

sabotage_vectors.attack: generateMicroFeedback catch block returns [] on any exception. Malformed frames (e.g. thermal_snapshots with wrong structure, NaN in angle_degrees) cause inner generators to throw. Catch swallows; returns empty array. User sees no micro-feedback; no error surfaced.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Verification tests valid frames only. No test with malformed frame structure. logWarn is fire-and-forget; no assertion checks for empty feedback when frames exist.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: Replay page shows no feedback for sessions with edge-case frame data; debugging requires inspecting logs

sabotage_vectors.attack: Gate 'if (!sessionData?.frames || !primaryScore) return []' prevents flicker by not rendering until both load. If primaryScore API returns 500 or times out, primaryScore stays undefined. microFeedback stays []. User sees no feedback indefinitely with no error state.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Verification says 'Micro-feedback does not re-render visibly when score loads after frames'. No handling for score never loading. No error UI for failed score fetch.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: Replay page with failed score fetch shows empty feedback; user may think session has no issues

sabotage_vectors.attack: test_put_threshold_invalidates_cache overrides get_db for sessions and thresholds. If thresholds router uses a different dependency name (e.g. get_db vs get_db_session), the override may not apply. Threshold route hits real DB; weld_thresholds empty; PUT 404. Test fails for wrong reason.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Plan says 'override get_db for routes.thresholds and routes.sessions'. Does not specify exact dependency override key. FastAPI uses function reference; route module may import get_db from different module.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: test_put_threshold_invalidates_cache flaky or fails; cache invalidation unverified

precondition_gaps: Step 1.7: create_session accepts any process_type string. No validation that process_type in (mig,tig,stick,flux_core). Invalid values stored; scoring falls back to mig.

Step 4.6: session_factory imports from data.mock_sessions. Tests assume PYTHONPATH or cwd includes backend. If test run from repo root without setup, import fails. Plan does not document test run requirements.
Step 1.1: WeldTypeThresholds.angle_target_degrees has ge=0. Backend requires >0. Model allows 0; creates inconsistency for programmatic creation outside PUT.
untestable_verifications: Step 4.2: 'verify markers don't visibly re-render when score loads after frames' — subjective. No DOM assertion, no timing check. A 50ms flash would pass for one reviewer and fail for another. Throttle test is manual; not automatable in CI.

Step 3.2: 'Arc spans correct angle (visual: 45° arc)' — verification is visual. No assertion of SVG path d attribute or computed angles. Wrong formula could render a 30° arc; visual check might miss it.
cascade_points: Step 1.2 migration: Single point of failure. If upgrade fails or is partially applied, weld_thresholds table or process_type column missing. All of Phase 2 and 3 (backend) fail. Rollback documented but partial migration recovery (e.g. UPDATE succeeded, ALTER failed) not.

Step 2.2 score_session: All scoring flows through it. If thresholds param passed incorrectly (e.g. wrong weld_type mapping), every score uses wrong thresholds. test_put_threshold_invalidates_cache verifies cache; test_scoring_thresholds verifies one TIG case. No exhaustive process_type matrix.
phase_boundary_gaps: Phase 2 to Phase 3: Backend deploys before frontend. If frontend expects full active_threshold_spec (thermal, amps, volts, heat_diss) and backend returns legacy 4-field spec during deploy race, Replay uses fallbacks (20, 40 for thermal). TIG should use 60. Wrong micro-feedback until backend fully deployed.

Phase 1 to Phase 2: test_put_threshold_invalidates_cache is in Step 4.6 (Phase 4) but Phase 2 prereq says 'run early to verify cache invalidation'. No explicit Phase 1 exit criterion that this test passes. Implementer may skip.

pre_mortem.partial_execution: We got through Phase 1 and Phase 2 but had to stop. The state of the codebase was: migration 004 applied, weld_thresholds seeded, threshold_service and score route wired. Phase 3 (admin UI) was not started. Backfill script (Step 1.8) was updated. prototype_arc_scoring.py and prototype_aggregate_perf.py were never updated. A dev ran prototype_aggregate_perf.py after seeding a TIG session; output showed MIG-based scores. Root cause: plan updates backfill but not prototype scripts.
pre_mortem.silent_corruption: Everything appeared to work. Users reported issues 3 days later. The root cause was: create_session accepted process_type='tig' from a client typo (e.g. 't1g'). Stored in DB. get_thresholds('t1g') not in cache, falls back to mig. All such sessions scored with MIG. WelderReport showed 'Evaluated against MIG spec' for sessions that should be TIG. No validation at session creation.
pre_mortem.prod_vs_local: The staging deployment revealed: tests passed (SQLite, create_all). Production migration 004 failed with 'column process_type already exists' because a previous partial run had added the column but not completed. Alembic revision table was inconsistent. Migration was never run in CI against a real DB.
pre_mortem.dependency_drift: Three months later, when Pydantic 2.x updated validation behaviour for ge/le on floats, WeldTypeThresholds(angle_target_degrees=0) began raising instead of passing. A script that had relied on 0 for 'unset' broke. The plan allowed ge=0; we had added ad-hoc 0 checks in PUT. Drift between model and API validation surfaced.

critical_issues: Step 1.7: create_session does not validate process_type. Invalid values (e.g. 'submerged_arc', typos) stored. All such sessions scored with MIG. Add validation: reject if process_type not in (mig,tig,stick,flux_core).

Plan omits updates to prototype_arc_scoring.py and prototype_aggregate_perf.py. They call extract_features(session) and score_session(session, features) with no thresholds. If run with TIG/stick/flux_core sessions in DB, output is wrong. Add step or document as known limitation.
latent_issues: Step 1.1: WeldTypeThresholds.angle_target_degrees ge=0 allows 0. Backend PUT rejects 0. Model should use gt=0 to match. Trigger: programmatic creation of WeldTypeThresholds with 0.
Step 1.2: Migration never run in CI. Trigger: PostgreSQL-specific migration failure on first prod deploy.
Step 4.1: generateMicroFeedback catch returns []. Trigger: malformed frames cause exception; user sees no feedback.
Step 4.2: No error UI when score fetch fails. microFeedback stays []. Trigger: score API 500 or timeout.
blind_spots: Plan assumes prototype_arc_scoring.py and prototype_aggregate_perf.py are not in the critical path. They are dev/tuning scripts but use the same scoring stack. If TIG sessions exist, their output is wrong. Plan does not mention them.
Plan assumes DATABASE_URL is writable. Read replicas are common in staging. Pre-flight has no read-only check.
Plan assumes test client override applies to all routes that need DB. Dependency injection key must match. If thresholds router uses a differently-named dependency, override fails silently.
minor_issues: Step 3.4: Tab switch with unsaved edits overwrites form state. No 'unsaved changes' prompt. UX irritant.
Step 4.5: Hardcoded fallback 'MIG spec — Target 45° ±5°' in demo. Note says it can be wrong if admin changes MIG. Minor for demo.
what_the_author_cant_see: The plan was written with primary flows in mind: migration, threshold service, score route, admin UI, Replay. The author updated backfill_score_total.py because it was in the 'score sessions' path. prototype_arc_scoring.py and prototype_aggregate_perf.py are in a different mental bucket — dev tools. They never appeared in the dependency graph. The same gap: create_session validation. The author focused on PUT validation (angle_target_degrees=0) and form validation. Session creation was 'add optional field, default mig'. Validating process_type at creation didn't occur because the mental model was 'sessions come from controlled sources'. Production has typos and future integrations.
minimum_fixes_before_execution: Add process_type validation to create_session: reject if not in (mig,tig,stick,flux_core) with 422.
Change WeldTypeThresholds.angle_target_degrees to gt=0 (or add WeldThresholdUpdate with gt=0) for consistency.

Add plan step or Known Issues entry: prototype_arc_scoring.py and prototype_aggregate_perf.py use legacy score_session/extract_features; will score with MIG constants if non-MIG sessions exist. Update or document.
Add Phase 1 CI checkpoint: run alembic upgrade head against SQLite (or ephemeral Postgres) in CI to catch migration syntax errors.
Specify exact FastAPI dependency override for test client (e.g. routes.thresholds.get_db, routes.sessions.get_db).
